Tests if multiplying `Number.NaN` by `Number.MAX_VALUE` results in `NaN`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple test assertion that checks the behavior of JavaScript's `Number` object when multiplying `Number.NaN` by `Number.MAX_VALUE`. The assertion uses the `isNaN` function to verify that the result of this multiplication is `NaN`, and it expects the expression to evaluate to `true`. This test ensures that the multiplication of `NaN` with any number, including the maximum possible number in JavaScript, results in `NaN`, which is consistent with the IEEE 754 standard for floating-point arithmetic. The code provides narrow functionality, focusing on a specific aspect of JavaScript's number handling.
